title: Avatar setting in User Profile
excerpt: We are using an Enterprise account with SSO via SCIM.  Our users are having difficulty setting their User Icons.  Upon logging in their only option is to upload a profile pic.  No option is presented to set just their in…

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml:
We are using an Enterprise account with SSO via SCIM. Our users are having difficulty setting their User Icons. Upon logging in their only option is to upload a profile pic. No option is presented to set just their initials. The “Change avatar them” option is not available to them. A workaround that seems to work is to temporarily upload a file using ‘Upload profile picture’ and then remove it. At that point, their user icon changes to their initials and the ‘Change avatar them’ is then available to them. They can then use it to select the background color for their initials.

While this is a relatively minor issue, it is very inconvenient for our account as until they get something set the user icons across the full organization look exactly the same. The initials option/‘Change avatar theme’ not being available without this work around seems to be a bug.

posts.author: Kkolman (Kristen Kolman)
posts.cookedHtml: This is currently happening in our account as well. Support ticket requested 2/5/25.

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, this behavior has never changed in our account. We continue to have to walk users through a step by step just so that we can tell them apart in their avatars.

posts.author: Wer4 (Alexander )
posts.cookedHtml: Apart disconnect services of avatar ,alias,etc , erase system remove default

posts.author: hbristol (Heather Bristol)
posts.cookedHtml: @Wer4 if this is a work around suggestion I’m not following?
